data.title: Nvidia, Broadcom 'ripe for outperformance' in 2026, Cantor Fitzgerald says
data.description:
Semiconductor giants Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) appear to be “ripe for outperformance” in 2026, after a recent bout of weakness, Cantor Fitzgerald said on Wednesday.

“The AI trade has obviously been a key debate area lately, with the AI levered group pressured since early November as we entered a more risk-off environment with AI bubble fears at the forefront,” Cantor Fitzgerald analyst C.J. Muse wrote in a note to clients. “We continue to believe that these fears are overdone and shortsightedly missing the forest for the trees as we remain at the cusp of a massive AI- related demand inflection.”

For Nvidia, shares are trading at just 16 times Cantor's earnings estimates, even as Blackwell-trained large language models are just starting to occur, and a rack-scale “supercycle” with Blackwell and the upcoming Vera Rubin line of GPUs kicking into gear.

Broadcom is trading at 26 times Cantor's earnings estimates, even as Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) tensor processing units continue to ramp up, which should drive “positive sentiment,” Muse added. Additionally, Muse said Broadcom's networking exposure is also benefiting from growing AI infrastructure spending.

“So, we are highly confident in reiterating NVDA and AVGO as [top picks] into CY26 and would recommend building positions sooner-rather-than-later,” Muse added.

data.title: Santa rally leaves bitcoin behind
data.description:
While year-end cheer fuels rallies in traditional markets, bitcoin (BTC-USD) remains stuck near $87K, still struggling to regain momentum after peaking at record highs in October.

The world's largest cryptocurrency (BTC-UDS) is on pace to close out 2025 with an ~8% slide as of Wednesday morning trading, bucking the S&P 500 Index's 18% climb and gold's (XAUUSD:CUR) 70% surge. Ether (ETH-USD) retreated 13% to $2.9K year-to-date, with many other major altcoins tracking double-digit losses.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) falling markedly behind leaves its role in portfolios unclear. U.S. stocks are doing their part as risk-on assets, while gold is reinforcing its role as both an uncertainty hedge and a store of value. Where does that leave BTC?

Bitget Wallet analyst Lacie Zhang contended that a bullish equities backdrop in the final days of the year might spill into crypto, which "often track broader risk assets during seasonal rebounds." She predicted that bitcoin (BTC-USD) will likely change hands within the $86K-$93K range in the near term, while ether (ETH-USD) could trade at $2.8K-$3.2K.

It will be interesting to see if bitcoin (BTC-USD) makes any notable moves over Christmas while U.S. stock markets are closed.

Citi Research has contended that bitcoin's (BTC-USD) performance this year -- hampered in part by leveraged long liquidations and slowed flows across exchange-traded funs -- will reverse in 2026 on the view that greater regulatory clarity will continue to boost digital asset adoption.

data.title: C.H. Robinson is the AI leader in the global logistics sector - BofA
data.description:
Bank of America reiterated its bullish stance on C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) on its view that the global logistics company is widening its moat.

Analyst Ken Hoexter and his team see an improving outlook for C.H. Robinson (CHRW) in comparison to prior estimates due to continued cost controls. While recognizing the impact of rising spot rates on brokerage businesses, CHRW is seen working around the headwinds from its use of AI to take share, improve gross margins, and reduce its employee base. "It has proven that capability over the past 2 years, and this test of rising rates will show its relative outperformance," wrote Hoexter.

In the C-suite, new CEO Dave Bozeman was said to be driving an overhaul of CHRW as it launches a new operating model, drives systemic improvements, and aims to benefit from its status as the largest truck brokerage provider with scale benefits while still balancing results with volume growth. "It is taking advantage of a sustained loose trucking market and improved ocean shipping market given Red Sea disruptions globally. Given that, we can see valuation return to premium levels," highlighted Hoexter.

The Seeking Alpha Quant rating on CHRW is flashing Buy, and the quant score is the second highest in the air freight and logistics sector.

data.title: Edgewise rises on mid-stage trial data for heart disease therapy
data.description:
Edgewise Therapeutics (EWTX) added ~17% on Wednesday after the Boulder, Colorado-based biopharma updated data from a mid-stage trial for EDG-7500, its experimental therapy for a genetic heart disorder known as hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

Citing interim data from the ongoing Part D portion of its CIRRUS-HCM Phase 2 trial, Edgewise (EWTX) said that as of Dec. 23, EDG-7500 demonstrated a favorable and well-tolerated safety profile. Consistent with prior findings, there were no clinically significant changes in left ventricular ejection fraction or reductions in LVEF to below 50%, according to the company.

In April, Edgewise (EWTX) posted initial results from Part B and C portions in which patients with obstructive or nonobstructive HCM showed improvements in HCM disease markers after receiving 50 mg and 100 mg of EDG-7500, over four weeks, respectively.

New data from patients who received a lower 25 mg fixed dose in Cohorts B and C also showed a consistent safety profile and clinical activity across key HCM disease markers, the company said, noting that both cohorts have now completed dosing.

"I'm excited about the advances we've made in Part D of the CIRRUS-HCM trial, where we've exceeded our year-end enrollment goal, highlighting continued enthusiasm for the program from patients and physicians," noted CEO Kevin Koch.

The company plans to release a full data readout from Part D in Q2 2026 before beginning a late-stage trial for EDG-7500 by the end of next year.

data.title: Picard Medical to raise up to $50 million senior secured debt financing
data.description:
• Picard Medical (PMI) said on Wednesday it has entered into a definitive agreement for a private placement financing of up to $50 million in senior secured notes due 2028, along with warrants to purchase common stock.
  • The company expects to issue an initial $15 million of notes at closing, with the option to raise up to an additional $35 million in one or more tranches, subject to certain conditions. The notes will be senior secured obligations.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • PMI is -3.73% to $2.06.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ulta's 'Unleashed' strategy heads into a payoff year in 2026
data.description:
After a year of investments and strategic initiatives that included an end to its Target (TGT) partnership and acquisition of the UK’s Space NK, Ulta Beauty (ULTA) is setting up 2026 to enjoy the fruits of its “Ulta Beauty Unleashed” initiative.

Launched in 2025, the initiative was a strategic plan to accelerate growth by enhancing in-store experiences, launching the UB Marketplace for diverse brands, and to diversify its global footprint with new locations in Mexico and the Middle East.

Ulta (ULTA) is also capitalizing on the convergence of beauty and wellness. This year, the company tripled shelf space in over 400 stores with offerings from Nutrafol, Lemme, and Ritual, along with aromatherapy merchandise, skincare tools, and bath & body products, contributing to a 950% surge in comparable store sales from a year ago and more than double what Wall Street expected.

“We believe the convergence is driven by a shift in consumer mindset from reactive health to proactive wellness, which is reshaping how consumers engage with the category,” said Jefferies Ashley Helgans.

“In wellness, we’re focused on leveraging our position as a trusted guide to expand more meaningfully into the vast and growing wellness category,” Ulta CEO Kecia Steelman said on the company’s most recent earnings call.

As the company adjusts its investments in 2026 to align with sales growth, Ulta (ULTA) is expected to deliver modest EBIT margin expansion next year. Morgan Stanley models ~12.5% EBIT margin in 2026, a gain of 10 basis points year-over-year. The company is also expected to “optimize promotions” to give a further boost to margins.

“We think it shows Ulta is continuing to optimize its promotional events with greater targeting across customers, channels, and relative to competitors,” adds UBS’s Michael Lasser.

A successful holiday season should result in strong fourth quarter results, and by extension, a solid performance in 2026 despite the inevitable post-Christmas slump most retailers experience. Ulta’s (ULTA) differentiated offering, value proposition in the beauty category, and loyal customer base will provide a durable foundation to help offset any seasonal volatility.

For 2026, Ulta (ULTA) expects sales to increase to $12.3B with comparable store sales rising 4.4% to 4.7%, an increase from its prior targets of $12.06B and 2.5%-3.5%, respectively. This will likely drive a profit of $25.20 to $25.50 per share.

Crucially, operating margins are projected to be better than initially expected, now within a range of 12.3% to 12.4% from 11.9% to 12.0%, previously.

“After several years of laying new foundations, the payback period is finally arriving, it seems. With shrink still trending lower, Marketplace just getting started, international doors ramping, and a healthier newness pipeline, I believe the underlying playbook is actually improving even if the consumer environment stays uneven,” says Seeking Alpha analyst Sandpiper Investment Research.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors all share this upbeat outlook on Ulta (ULTA), viewing the stock as a Buy. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating is more cautious, however, viewing Ulta (ULTA) as a Hold, albeit with a Quant score of 3.41 out of 5.

data.title: China's Jiangxi Copper to buy SolGold in $1 billion deal
data.description:
Jiangxi Copper (JIAXF) reached an agreement to acquire SolGold (SLGGF), valuing the copper developer at just over $1 billion as competition for scarce copper assets intensifies.

SolGold (SLGGF) said Wednesday that it had accepted Jiangxi’s latest cash offer of 28 pence per share for the stock the Chinese group does not already own, after rejecting two earlier proposals. Including potential new shares, the transaction values the company at up to £867 million, or about $1.17 billion. The bid represents a roughly 43% premium to SolGold’s (SLGGF) share price prior to Jiangxi’s initial approach last month.

The takeover agreement comes as copper prices hover near record highs, fueling expectations of future supply shortages as electrification accelerates globally. That outlook has sparked renewed dealmaking interest across the mining sector, even as some high-profile efforts, such as BHP Group’s (BHP) (BHPLF) unsuccessful pursuit of Anglo American (AAUKF) (NGLOY), have faltered.

SolGold (SLGGF) had previously been viewed as a likely acquisition target for large Western miners, including BHP (BHP) (BHPLF) and Newmont (NEM), both of which rank among its largest shareholders. However, disputes over financing plans for its flagship Cascabel copper project in Ecuador, along with a scaling back of the development, cooled earlier takeover interest.

Jiangxi Copper is currently SolGold’s (SLGGF) single largest shareholder, holding slightly more than 12%. SolGold (SLGGF) said both BHP (BHP) (BHPLF) and Newmont (NEM), each with stakes exceeding 10%, have indicated their support for the deal. Other investors, including Maxit Capital, have also backed the transaction.

Beyond Cascabel, SolGold (SLGGF) controls extensive exploration acreage across Ecuador, positioning the company as a long-term play on copper supply at a time when the metal is increasingly viewed as critical to energy transition infrastructure.

data.title: Wall Street is muted on the holiday shortened Christmas Eve session
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages were muted on Wednesday, kicking off a shortened regular trading session due to Christmas Eve.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last -0.1%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was near even, and the blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) was +0.1%. 

From a sector vantage point, eight of the S&P 500’s 11 sectors were in the green with health care at the top of the list. Going the other way, communication services was the weakest performer so far. 

U.S. Treasury yields were slightly lower. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved down 1 basis point to 3.53%, while the U.S.10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dipped 1 basis point to 4.15%. 

On the economic front, traders received some labor market data in the form of an unexpectedly lower initial jobless claims report. 

A blowout U.S. Q3 GDP report lifted sentiment in the previous session and helped Wall Street notch its 38th record close of the year, underscoring the stock market's resilience after a turbulent 2025. 

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of Nike (NKE) were +4.7% while Intel (INTC) were -1.17%.

For reference, markets will close at 13:00 ET today and will be closed on Thursday.

data.title: Stocks little changed at the open in truncated Christmas Eve trading session
data.description:
U.S. stocks opened largely flat on Wednesday, kicking off a shortened regular trading session due to Christmas Eve.

Markets will close at 1300 ET today and will be shut on Thursday.

The Dow (DJI) rose +0.05% to 48,467.49 points, while the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was down -0.01% at 6,909.23 points. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) fell -0.09% to 23,541.38 points.

Traders received some labor market data in the form of an unexpectedly lower initial jobless claims report. 

A blowout U.S. Q3 GDP report lifted sentiment in the previous session and helped Wall Street notch its 38th record close of the year, underscoring the stock market's resilience after a turbulent 2025.

See the chart below shared by Charlie Bilello, chief market strategist at Creative Planning, on X:

Wednesday also marks the start of a period that has traditionally resulted in gains for equities, leading to a Santa Claus rally - a historical trend that has seen the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) post gains during the last five trading days of the year and the first two of the next year.

Treasury yields showed mixed movement in early trading. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) ticked up to 3.54%, while the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) slipped to 4.16%. The longer-dated 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) also edged lower to 4.82%.

Top gainers included Nike (NKE) +3.99%, Sandisk (SNDK) +3.32%, and Danaher (DHR) +1.24%. Decliners included Paycom Software (PAYC) -3.50%, Intel (INTC) -2.86%, and Datadog (DDOG) -2.85%.
